claim: VERIFIED: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient to support PRD readiness. All claims are backed by project-scoped evidence from code and tests. No assumptions are presented as facts. Missing data is properly identified.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch artifact root contains explorer and cross-checker results with 22 total evidence citations from project code and tests
evidence.why: Evidence gathering completed and persisted to artifact store

evidence.summary: Boundary audit implementation verified by both agents: SHA-256 digests, before/after comparison, blocker generation
evidence.why: Core claim supported by direct code evidence at cited lines

evidence.summary: PRD readiness inspection enforces evidence quality via evidenceResultBlockingIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues()
evidence.why: Router logic verifies evidence protocol is enforced

evidence.summary: Tests confirm boundary blocks mutations even when agent results pass, including untracked files
evidence.why: Behavioral verification that boundary audit works as specified

evidence.summary: Provider adapter supports full-tool agent execution (Write/Bash tools available)
evidence.why: Verifies agents have full tool access while boundary audit prevents mutations

evidence.summary: Explorer result: 10 evidence pieces, 0 assumptions, 3 risks, 3 missing items identified
evidence.why: Explorer completed with comprehensive evidence and no assumptions promoted to facts

evidence.summary: Cross-checker result: 12 evidence pieces, independent verification of all explorer claims
evidence.why: Cross-checker independently confirmed explorer evidence without conflicts

risks: Evidence confirms boundary audit works in tested scenarios, but large repos (>10k files) performance is unverified
Concurrent dispatches to same project root could race if snapshots overlap
Subprocess mutations from agents may not be detected if they occur outside snapshot window
missing: Evidence that boundary audit handles filesystem errors during snapshot creation gracefully
Performance benchmarks for boundary snapshot creation on repos with >10k files
Evidence that concurrent dispatches to same project are serialized or isolated

result.notes: Verification protocol satisfied: (1) No assumptions presented as facts - all evidence is project-scoped from code/tests. (2) Evidence paths are usable and verified - 22 citations across src/demand/, src/runtime/, and __tests__/. (3) Missing data is properly identified in missing/risks arrays and not promoted to facts. Acceptance criteria met: all three agents cite project paths, boundary audit keeps PRD blocked on mutations, evidence protocol enforced via router. Ready for PRD phase with acknowledged gaps in performance and concurrent execution scenarios.
